key_evidence: DOJ criminal division expanded its probe beyond Medicare billing to Optum Rx and physician-compensation practices (Fierce Healthcare, PYMNTS)
UnitedHealth reversed its prior stance to confirm it is cooperating with the DOJ investigation (StarTribune)
CEO Andrew Witty departed abruptly mid-May amid the active criminal investigation; former CEO Stephen Hemsley returned
AI-claim-denial class action survived motion to dismiss — claims deemed plausible enough to proceed (Lawfold)
Multi-agency exposure (DOJ, HHS, SEC, FTC); stock down ~55% from all-time high

events.label: CEO departure during expanding DOJ criminal probe + AI-claim-denial suit survives MTD

methodology_note: Dimension-specific governance downgrade: ACC 1.25->1.125 (cooperation only after reversal; probe expanding; no proactive harm acknowledgment) and INT 1.625->1.375 (abrupt CEO exit under criminal investigation; AI-denial suit survives MTD). Reconstruction of published dims = 11.3 (diff 0.1 from stored 11.4, within tolerance, no math-hygiene flag). Delta -1.2 sub-5pt; dimension-specific. Remains Critical.
